snippets.Angular Cancellation Lemma: establish global regularity for the three-dimensional incompressible Navier–Stokes equations on the periodic domain T3 for arbitrary smooth initial data. The proof introduces three new structural mechanisms. snippets.Theorem 4.27: the three-dimensional incompressible Navier–Stokes equations on the periodic domain T3 for arbitrary smooth initial data. The proof introduces three new structural mechanisms. First, the Angular Cancellation Lemma (ACL, Theorem 4.27) provides a sharp bound on the local cascade interaction within each Littlewood– Paley shell, establishing that azimuthal oscillation of the Leray-projected coupling kernel produces deterministic geometric cancellation.

snippets.Theorem 2.2: a field-level equivariance statement by using a quantitative axis-equivariance defect; Appendix K and Appendix L record the absolute estimates behind the flux-hull routing. The reader may first follow the proof through Theorem 2.2, treating these estimates as lemmas, and then return to the appendices for the line-by-line inequalities. snippets.axisymmetric-with-swirl: is a reduction theorem: any hypothetical first singularity of a general three-dimensional solution generates, after scale normalization, threshold selection, and endpoint extraction, a terminal singular endpoint in the axisymmetric-with-swirl class. Thus a proof of the companion axisymmetricwith-swirl endpoint theorem closes the corresponding full three-dimensional finite-energy regularity statement.
